ads.body:
✨🎉💚💛 Happy New Year to the ZKTC Community 💛💚🎉✨

As we step into the new year, we’re proud to share several major milestones with our community.

The private presale has officially concluded, and we’re grateful to announce that $22,157 in charitable donations were raised during this phase — scheduled for deployment beginning March 2026 alongside our public launch.

🚀💚💛 Public Presale Is Now Open
• Minimum to acquire ZKTC: $2,500
• Request your unlock code today

🤝💚💛 First Official ZakatRated Charity Partner
We are proud to announce Human Development Fund (HDF) as the first officially ZakatRated charity in the Zakat Coin ecosystem — a globally trusted organization delivering humanitarian aid and Zakat worldwide.

📲💚💛 ZakPay Is Live
ZakPay is now available on iOS & Android, enabling real utility today.
• KYC supported in 18+ countries

🏦💚💛 Coinbase On-Ramping Coming Soon
ACH, Apple Pay, and debit card support will be going live in the coming days.

📅💚💛 Public Launch Countdown
🗓 March 26, 2026

This is only the beginning. Thank you for believing in ethical, utility-first blockchain built for real-world impact.

🌍 https://zktcoin.com #ZakatCoin #ZKTC #ZakPay #ZakatRated
#HumanDevelopmentFund CryptoGiving
EthicalBlockchain IslamicFinance
Web3ForGood Zakat Sadaqah

ads.title: Coinbase is your way out

ads.body:
💰 Bitcoin is one of the most valuable assets in the world and its inception in 2008 paved way for creating a market capitalisation of $1.57 trillion. Despite its limited supply of only around 21 million coins, some the planet’s largest organisations, people, and governments own vast amounts.

📈 Satoshi Nakamoto, the pseudonym of the creator, is by far the largest holder with 1.1 million coins. Several crypto exchange companies, such as Coinbase, follow behind him with each holding hundreds of thousands of coins. 

🤑 Private individuals such as the Winklevoss twins purchased their coins in the early days of Bitcoin, when the price was much lower than today. Governments, such as the U.S. and UK, largely obtained their coins from various crime seizures. 

Follow @move for interesting stories, amazing places, and incredible people 💫

#Move #Bitcoin #Crypto #Finance #Business

ads.title: You can now trade on whatever you're into with prediction markets on Coinbase.

messaging_angles: Multi-coin accessibility (Bitcoin, Ethereum, Solana, XRP, Dogecoin)
messaging_analysis.buy_crypto_framing: direct — messaging is action-oriented with 'Ready to buy?' and direct CTAs to download/install, pushing immediate conversion rather than education
messaging_analysis.fees_messaging: completely absent — no mention of fees, costs, or pricing in any ads
messaging_analysis.simplicity_messaging: heavily emphasized through repetitive '[Coinbase] Makes [Crypto] Easy' format and 'getting started easy' messaging, positioning ease-of-use as the core value proposition
messaging_analysis.security_messaging: not emphasized — no explicit trust, security, or safety messaging present in these ads
messaging_analysis.dominant_format: video — vast majority of active, long-running ads are video format
longest_running_themes: Multi-coin positioning (Bitcoin, Ethereum, Solana, etc.) — 170+ days
messaging_analysis.summary: This competitor (Coinbase) relies heavily on simplicity messaging with a formulaic '[Coin Name] Made Easy' approach across multiple cryptocurrencies, running primarily video ads for 170+ days. The strategy is direct-conversion focused with 'Ready to buy?' calls and download CTAs, positioning crypto access as simple while completely avoiding fee discussions and framing the product as an escape from traditional finance.

gaps: No multi-use case messaging (competitor segments by coin type, Speed could segment by use case: remittance, gaming, everyday spending)
No 'getting started' or onboarding-focused messaging to reduce friction for crypto-curious users
No aspirational or alternative finance framing (no 'future of money' or anti-establishment angle)
Missing 'ready to act' purchase-readiness framing that creates urgency
No video ad format mentioned in current copy examples
Lack of repetitive, formulaic messaging that builds brand recognition across segments
speed_advantages: Zero fees (competitor completely avoids fee messaging, this is Speed's killer differentiator)
Instant deposits for specific use case (iGaming) - competitor has no vertical-specific positioning
Lightning Network technology as a tangible infrastructure advantage vs generic 'easy' claims
Rewards program (Speed Stacks) - competitor shows no rewards messaging
Clear remittance value proposition with concrete savings angle
angles_to_test: Formulaic simplicity series: 'Speed Makes [Remittances] Easy', 'Speed Makes [Gaming Deposits] Easy', 'Speed Makes [Bitcoin] Easy'
Purchase-readiness framing: 'Ready to send money home?', 'Ready to deposit instantly?', 'Ready to use Bitcoin?'
Onboarding reducer: 'Your first Bitcoin payment in 60 seconds', 'Getting started is easy: Download, fund, send'
Multi-use case creative: Separate ad sets showcasing Bitcoin for remittance vs gaming vs everyday use
Alternative finance angle with Speed's advantage: 'Banks charge fees. Speed doesn't.' or 'Skip Western Union. Use Bitcoin Lightning.'
Feature-forward simplicity: 'Instant Bitcoin payments. Zero fees. That's it.'
avoid: Vague 'makes crypto easy' claims without specific proof points (Speed should lead with tangible benefits: zero fees, instant speed)
Ignoring fee messaging (competitor avoids it, Speed should weaponize it)
Generic multi-coin hedging without conviction (Speed is Bitcoin Lightning-focused, own it)
Pure download CTAs without context (Speed's CTAs should reinforce the core benefit: 'Download Speed. Send free.' not just 'Download')
speed_comparison.recommendations: Test formulaic simplicity messaging across all three segments with use-case specific variants ('Speed Makes Remittances Easy/Free'). Always anchor simplicity claims to your zero-fee differentiator since competitors avoid fee discussions entirely. Develop video creative showing the 60-second onboarding flow for crypto-curious users, directly countering complexity concerns. Create purchase-readiness campaigns for each segment ('Ready to send money home? Ready to deposit instantly?') with segment-specific CTAs that reinforce the core benefit.
